scope_contract: In --changed-files mode this inspector emits findings only for the listed files and tags each with introduced_in_diff:true|false per AC-2.6 default-safe rule.

domain_check.rationale: All three cycle-diff files are Python source (hook implementation + test code), not command/agent Markdown documentation. Prompt-inspector audits .md files for verbose explanatory sections (## Philosophy, ## Overview, ## Quality Standards, etc.); Python docstrings and comments are outside the declared role scope. Per orchestrator instruction: when no .md files appear in --changed-files, return findings=[] with an out-of-domain note rather than synthesizing out-of-scope findings.

summary.note: No Markdown command/agent documentation files in this cycle's diff. No verbosity inspection performed. Default-safe rule: absence of findings is not interpreted as cleanliness assertion over .py files.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex returned 5 OBSERVATION_ONLY items and zero PROPOSED_FIXes. Codex confirmed: (1) draft plan is correct given the orchestrator's explicit out-of-domain instruction; (2) Python docstrings/comments are out of prompt-inspector scope even if they contain Markdown-style headers; (3) opening .py files for verbosity inspection would risk inventing out-of-scope findings; (4) scanning full repo command/agent docs would be noisy and contrary to --changed-files mode contract; (5) the theoretical false-negative (embedded prompt-like Markdown inside Python) is acceptable and intentional under current role definition — any such expansion belongs in a separate inspector, not this cycle's prompt-inspector behavior.

findings.issue: Draft plan validation: findings=[], status=out-of-domain, brief note
findings.rationale: Codex confirmed the draft plan is correct and compliant.

findings.issue: Python docstrings with Markdown-style headers theoretically could contain verbose sections
findings.rationale: Out of declared prompt-inspector scope; would require role-scope expansion, not in-cycle behavior change.

findings.issue: Whether to open .py files defensively for embedded Markdown blocks
findings.rationale: Codex advised against; risks inventing out-of-scope findings.

findings.issue: Whether to scan full-repo .md docs and tag introduced_in_diff:false
findings.rationale: Codex advised against in --changed-files mode; would be noisy and contrary to mode contract.

findings.issue: Acceptable false-negative risk on theoretically-embedded prompt Markdown inside Python
findings.rationale: Codex marked the residual risk acceptable and intentional under current role definition.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 5 codex items were OBSERVATION_ONLY with no PROPOSED_FIX. No changes required to draft plan. Plan emitted as-is: findings=[], status=out_of_domain_no_op, no .py file inspection, no full-repo .md scan.
